key_points: Covers adoption rates, market share, productivity metrics, and tool-specific stats

84% of developers use or plan to use AI tools

62% of developers rely on at least one AI coding assistant
51% of professional developers use AI tools daily
Only 29% of developers trust AI tool output (down from 70%+ in 2023)
JetBrains January 2026 AI Pulse survey data
90% of developers regularly use at least one AI tool at work
74% adopted specialized AI tools (coding assistants/agents beyond chatbots)
GitHub Copilot 29%, Cursor 18% among tools used at work
25+ statistics on AI in software development
85% of developers regularly use AI for coding, debugging, code review
AI could drive 30-35% productivity gains across the development process
97% of enterprise developers use generative AI coding tools daily
Top 100 AI pair programming statistics focusing on GitHub Copilot adoption
Developers complete tasks 55% faster using GitHub Copilot
Developers complete 126% more projects per week with Copilot vs manual coding
GitHub Copilot reached 4.7M paid subscribers in Jan 2026 (75% YoY growth)

stats.fact: 84% of developers use or plan to use AI tools in development, up from 76% in 2024
85% of developers regularly use AI tools for coding and software design
90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work by January 2026
74% of developers worldwide had adopted specialized AI developer tools by January 2026
51% of professional developers now use AI tools every single day
stats.source: Stack Overflow / JetBrains combined survey data 2025-2026

stats.fact: GitHub Copilot holds approximately 42% market share among paid AI coding tools
GitHub Copilot reached 4.7 million paid subscribers in January 2026 — 75% year-over-year growth
GitHub Copilot has approximately 20 million total users (paid + free) as of July 2025
90% of Fortune 100 companies have adopted GitHub Copilot as of early 2025
Cursor crossed $1B annualized revenue in late 2025, passing $2B ARR in Q1 2026
stats.source: Cursor company announcements / Pragmatic Engineer analysis

stats.fact: Cursor reached more than 1 million daily active users by 2025
Claude Code (28%) and Cursor (24%) account for over half of primary-tool selections
Claude Code usage at work grew 6x from ~3% in April–June 2025 to 18% by early 2026
stats.source: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey tracking data

stats.fact: Over 51% of all code committed to GitHub in early 2026 was AI-generated or AI-assisted
46%+ of newly written code is AI-assisted, projected to reach 60% by end of 2026
Developers save approximately 3.6 hours per week using AI coding tools
Developers complete coding tasks 55% faster using GitHub Copilot
Healthy ROI on AI coding tools is 2.5–3.5x on average; top-quartile organizations reach 4–6x
A Fortune 100 retailer saved over 450,000 developer hours using AI code review agents in one year (~50 hours/developer/month)
Only 29% of developers trust AI tool output — down from 70%+ in 2023
66% of developers cite 'AI solutions that are almost right, but not quite' as their biggest frustration
52% of respondents don't trust AI agent outputs enough to let them act autonomously
45% of AI-generated code introduces known security flaws
AI-generated code produces 10,000+ new security findings per month — 10x increase from late 2024
stats.source: Sonar State of Code Developer Survey 2026

stats.fact: 38% of employees shared confidential company data with unapproved AI systems
AI coding tools market reached $12.8 billion in 2026 (up from $5.1 billion in 2024)
AI coding tools market projected to reach $23.97 billion by 2030
stats.source: Virtue Market Research / Grand View Research

stats.fact: 78% of Fortune 500 companies have AI-assisted development in production, up from 42% in 2024
70% of developers use between two and four AI tools simultaneously; 15% use five or more
A METR randomized controlled trial found experienced open-source developers were 19% slower with AI tools despite feeling 20% faster

angles: The AI trust paradox: 84% adoption but only 29% trust — what's driving this gap and what it means for developers in 2026
Tool war analysis: How Cursor's meteoric rise (near-zero to $2B ARR in 18 months) challenges GitHub Copilot's dominance
Enterprise vs. indie developers: Why adoption rates, ROI, and risk profiles diverge so dramatically in 2026
The security time bomb: 45% of AI-generated code has known flaws — how teams are responding
Beyond GitHub Copilot: The multi-tool reality — 70% of devs use 2-4 tools simultaneously
Claude Code's 6x growth story: From 3% to 18% market penetration in under a year
The perceived vs. measured productivity paradox: Devs feel 20% faster but METR study shows 19% slower
AI agents in 2026: Still not mainstream (52% don't use them or stick to simpler tools) despite massive hype
The $12.8B market breakdown: Who's spending, on what, and where growth is concentrated
